title: Chicken jalfrezi
subtitle: Curry-house dry-fry with peppers, tomatoes, and green chilli.
excerpt: Strips of chicken cooked dry-fry style over high heat with onion, pepper, ginger, fresh tomatoes, and green chilli. The British curry-house favourite, brought home in 45 minutes. Hot, dry-ish gravy and pieces of vegetable still with bite.
sourceNotes: The dish grew out of Bengali bhuna technique adapted in the British-Asian curry-house tradition of the 1970s and 80s. The dry-fry, high-heat character is distinctive: it sits between a Chinese stir-fry and a classical Bengali bhuna.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to 3 months. Defrost overnight and reheat in a wide pan over medium heat with a splash of water to loosen.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The base masala (the spiced onion-tomato mixture) can be made up to 2 days ahead. Add the chicken and finish on the day.

glossaryTerms.definition: A Bengali technique meaning to fry or stir-fry. In curry terms, the bhuna is the deep, dry frying-down of spice paste with onion until the oil separates and the colour darkens. The bhuna stage is where the curry's depth is built.

content.text: Jalfrezi is the curry-house plate that gets ordered when somebody wants something hot but not sweet. The technique borrows from the bhuna: spice paste fried down hard in oil until the colour deepens. What separates jalfrezi is the pepper, the chunked tomato, and the high-heat stir of the finished dish, which keeps the vegetables with bite and the gravy short.
Use thigh, not breast. Strips cooked over a fierce heat seal and brown fast; breast turns dry inside two minutes.

items.prepNote: one finely chopped for the base, one cut into 2 cm wedges for the dry-fry
ripe, two chopped fine for the base, two cut into wedges
slit lengthways, one finely chopped, two whole

content.text: A wide heavy frying pan or a kadai-style curry pan. The pan needs space so the chicken sears rather than stews.

Heat 2 tablespoons of {{vegetable-oil}} of vegetable oil in the pan over medium-high. Add {{cumin-seeds}} of cumin seeds and let them crackle for 10 seconds. Tip in the finely chopped {{onion}} of onion and cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often, until deep gold and starting to catch. Stir in {{garlic}} of garlic and {{ginger-root}} of ginger for 1 minute.

Add {{coriander-ground}} of coriander, {{turmeric}} of turmeric, {{paprika-sweet}} of paprika, {{cayenne}} of cayenne, and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Stir for 30 seconds. Stir in {{tomato-puree}} of tomato purée and the 2 finely chopped tomatoes plus the chopped green chilli. Cook on medium for 8 minutes, mashing with a wooden spoon, until the mixture darkens to a deep brick red and the oil starts to separate at the edges of the pan.

Push the base to the side of the pan. Tip in the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the empty space and add {{chicken-thigh}} of chicken chunks in a single layer. Sear for 3 minutes without moving, then turn and cook 2 minutes more. Stir the base back through; the chicken should be browned on the outside and just cooked through.

Tip in the onion wedges, {{pepper-red}} of red pepper, {{pepper-green}} of green pepper, the tomato wedges, and the two whole slit chillies. Pour in 150 ml of water. Stir, cover, and cook for 5 minutes over medium heat until the peppers are tender but still bright. Uncover and bubble for 2 minutes to reduce. Stir in {{garam-masala}} of garam masala.
Squeeze in the juice of {{lemon}} of lemon. Scatter with {{coriander}} of chopped coriander. Serve with basmati rice or naan.

items.symptom: Curry pale and oniony, not deep red
items.fix: Cook the spiced tomato-onion base for the full 8 minutes until the oil separates and the colour deepens. Without this stage, the curry tastes raw.

items.cause: Too much water added at the dry-fry stage.
items.fix: Jalfrezi is a dry-ish dish. Use only 150 ml of water; uncover at the end to drive moisture off.

items.cause: Cooked too long at the end.
items.fix: Cover for 5 minutes, uncover for 2 more — no longer. The peppers should still have bite.

items.cause: Ground spices added too late or not bloomed.
items.fix: Add ground spices to the hot onion-garlic-ginger mix and stir for 30 seconds before the tomato goes in. The brief frying activates the flavour.

content.text: Chicken jalfrezi is a British-Asian invention rooted in Bengali bhuna technique. The curry-house version codified in Bradford and East London restaurants in the 1980s sits between an Indian bhuna and a Chinese stir-fry: high heat, dry pan, peppers that snap. Serve with pilau rice and a side of cucumber raita to cool the chilli.
